description: Driver mobile app interactions — authentication, order accept/reject, activity updates, and trip completion through the public API.

actors.name: Driver
actors.description: Driver using the mobile app to receive and execute orders.

actors.name: Platform
actors.description: API server managing driver sessions, order pings, and activity transitions.

fields.name: identity
fields.label: Driver login credential — email or phone number.

fields.name: phone
fields.label: Phone number for SMS verification login.

fields.name: verification_code
fields.label: One-time code sent via SMS for phone-based login.

fields.name: auth_token
fields.label: Bearer token issued after successful authentication.

fields.name: device_token
fields.label: Push notification token (FCM or APNS) registered for this device.

fields.name: order_id
fields.label: Identifier of the order the driver is acting on.

fields.name: activity_code
fields.label: Activity transition code (e.g., driver_enroute, arrived, in_progress, completed).

fields.name: organization_id
fields.label: Organization the driver wants to switch their session to.

rules.rule_01: A driver can log in with email + password, or phone + SMS verification code.
rules.rule_02: After successful login, a bearer token is issued and must be included in subsequent requests.
rules.rule_03: A driver must register their push notification device token so they can receive order pings.
rules.rule_04: When pinged with an order, the driver can accept (by updating activity) or ignore (ping times out).
rules.rule_05: In adhoc mode, the first driver to accept is assigned; subsequent accepts are rejected.
rules.rule_06: A driver advances the order through activities by calling update-activity with the next activity code.
rules.rule_07: The platform determines the valid next activity at each step; invalid activity codes are rejected.
rules.rule_08: A driver can belong to multiple organizations and switch between them during the session.
rules.rule_09: After completing or canceling an order, the driver's current_job is cleared.

given: driver provides valid identity and password
user account exists and is active

then.description: A personal access token is issued for the driver's session.

driver_login_password.result: Driver receives a bearer token for authenticated API calls.

given: driver provides a registered phone number

then.description: A one-time verification code is sent via SMS.

driver_login_phone_step1.result: Driver receives an SMS code to confirm their identity.

given: driver submits the correct SMS verification code

then.description: A personal access token is issued.

driver_login_phone_step2.result: Driver is authenticated and receives a bearer token.

given: driver provides a device push token and platform

then.description: Device token is stored and linked to the driver's user account.

device_registered.result: Driver will receive push notifications for order pings and updates.

given: driver receives an order ping notification
driver calls update-activity with the enroute activity code
order is still unaccepted (adhoc) or assigned to this driver

then.description: Driver is locked to the order (adhoc case).
Order is set as driver's active job.

order_accepted.result: Order progresses to driver_enroute; customer is notified.

given: driver ignores the ping or explicitly declines
order_rejected.result: Ping expires or is ignored; platform may re-ping other drivers or notify operator.

given: driver calls update-activity with a valid activity code
the code is the expected next state in the order's flow

then.description: Order status advances according to the configured flow.
A tracking status entry is created recording the transition with location and timestamp.

activity_updated.result: Order advances to the next stage; customer sees updated status.

given: driver requests to switch to a different organization

then.description: Driver's active company session is updated.

organization_switched.result: Driver is now operating under the new organization context.

given: credentials are invalid or user does not exist

errors.message: Authentication failed using the provided credentials.
No driver found for the provided identity.
A device token is required to register the device.
The specified organization could not be found.
You do not belong to this organization.

events.name: order.updated
events.description: Fired when driver advances the order activity.

events.name: driver.location_changed
events.description: Fired as driver sends continuous location updates.

related.reason: Driver app drives the activity transitions that move an order through its lifecycle.
Driver app sends GPS updates on a regular interval during active trips.
Driver receives order pings via dispatch notifications.
Drivers can belong to multiple organizations and switch between them.
All driver app interactions go through the versioned public REST API.

goals.description: Driver mobile app interactions — authentication, order accept/reject, activity updates, and trip completion through the public API.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts

constraints.description: Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

tradeoffs.reason: workflow steps must complete correctly before proceeding

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
